Empires & Puzzles Brimstone Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Hero Guide

Is Brimstone still hot or has his fire gone cold? We review the Construct family's fiery juggernaut. Discover how his Destructive Core and unique Elemental Defense Down fiends can still melt titans and defenses alike in the 2026 meta.
Brimstone Review: The Hellish Construct That Burns It All
Brimstone, The Walking Volcano: Core Meltdowns and Fiery Fiends
Brimstone is a 5-star Legendary Fire hero from the

family, released back in the summer of 2023. In an era where heroes were just starting to get incredibly wordy special skills, Brimstone arrived as a walking natural disaster. He is a "do-it-all" damage dealer who combines direct AoE hits, a unique charging mechanic, and fiends that make enemies allergic to Fire. While he isn't the shiny new toy anymore, his toolkit—specifically the Elemental Defense Down tied to his fiends—gives him a unique longevity that many pure damage dealers lack. He is the kind of hero who hits you, then his passive hits you, then his fiend bites you. It's a bad day to be an Ice hero.

Brimstone looks exactly like his name implies: a hulking, molten demon that crawled out of a volcano's worst nightmare. The art is intense, featuring a massive, muscular frame composed of cooling magma and jagged rock. His design screams "Raid Boss," not "Helpful Teammate." The bright, glowing orange of his core contrasts sharply with the dark, obsidion-like armor plating. It is a visually aggressive design that fits his Barbarian class perfectly. He doesn't look like he wants to talk about your feelings; he wants to set your house on fire.
Brimstone possesses stats that were top-tier upon his release and, thanks to some balance updates, remain respectable even in early 2026.
This is his standout stat. Leaning heavily into offense, this high base attack scales beautifully with his damage-dealing special and fiends.
These are solid survival stats. While he doesn't have the massive 2500+ HP pools of the absolute latest tanks, he is far from squishy.
In the current meta, his stats are "Good but not Broken." He can survive a hit or two from modern snipers, but he relies on killing the enemy before they kill him.
Infernal Flames is a multi-layered skill that provides utility far beyond simple damage.
200% damage to all is decent, though not earth-shattering by itself at Average speed.
This is the meat of the skill. These aren't just damaging parasites; they are
applicators. As long as the fiend is alive, the target has -54% Defense against Fire. This is huge for Titan scores and red stacks.
Because the defense down is tied to the fiend, it cannot be "cleansed" by a standard healer or cleanser (like Rigard). You
heal the fiend away to remove the debuff. This forces the attacker to bring significant healing power.
The +86% Defense against Ice is a nice cherry on top, making him and his team surprisingly tanky against blue stacks, which are the natural counter to him.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family brings a unique mechanic that effectively gives Brimstone a second special skill.
The Construct family doesn't have a traditional "family bonus" like +Atk or +Def for having multiple members. Instead, their identity is the
This is what makes Constructs scary. The core charges up every time Brimstone takes damage or receives a status effect. When he casts his special, the core detonates.
It deals additional damage to all enemies based on charge level, up to 180%.
This punishes the enemy for hitting him. If they chip away at him with tiles, they are just charging his nuke. Since this damage happens
he casts, it combines with his 200% base hit for a massive wallop.
A subtle but powerful passive. He is immune to effects that stop him from firing (like Silence or Mindless Attack). This makes him very reliable; if he gets the mana, he

A simple, effective boost. For a hero whose fiend damage and special skill damage rely entirely on his Attack stat, this is the best possible Aether Power. It ensures his fiends bite harder and his initial hit stings more.
Upon release, Brimstone was a monster. The combination of high damage and the uncleansable (via normal means) Elemental Defense Down made him a staple in defenses and a titan king.
While 200% base damage seems low compared to newer "Hit All" heroes who might do 300%+, the Construct Core passive bridges that gap significantly. His stats have aged gracefully thanks to updates.

He has transitioned from a "Must-Have Everywhere" hero to a "Titan Specialist & Offensive Powerhouse." In PvP, he is excellent in a red stack to soften up enemies with fiends, but he faces stiff competition from faster heroes. On Titans, he remains top-tier due to the permanent -54% Fire Defense Down provided the fiend stays alive (which it usually does on Titans).
As of early 2026, Brimstone does not have a costume.

This means he relies entirely on his base stats and limit break bonuses. While this puts him slightly behind heroes with the massive stat boosts from costumes, his base kit is strong enough to stand on its own.
Brimstone is a Barbarian, which grants the
talent (chance to apply bleed on basic attacks). This is negligible for his role. The stats are what matter.
Maximizes Special Skill damage, Core damage, and crucially, Fiend damage (which scales off Attack).
Slightly squishier, though his base stats are high enough to survive.
Keeps him alive longer to charge his core and fire. Good for tank/flank use.
Wastes his potential as a damage dealer. His fiends become less threatening.
path (Full Attack) is the way to go. His primary role is to deal damage and amplify damage against Green targets. Maximizing his attack makes his fiends a nightmare to deal with.
-54% Defense against Fire is massive. Because it's a fiend effect, it doesn't wear off after a few turns like standard debuffs; it stays until the fiend is healed away.
Hits with Special + Hits with Passive Core.
The Elemental Defense Down requires healing to remove, not a cleanse. This bypasses heroes who rely on "Cleanse Ailments" skills.
Immune to Special Skill blocking means he rarely misfires once charged.
In a world of Fast and Very Fast heroes, Average can feel sluggish without mana troops or rush format.
His utility (the debuff) is removed if the enemy has strong healers.
He offers pure damage and debuffs but no utility to protect his team from ailments or remove enemy buffs.
His absolute best role. The EDD is invaluable for high scores against Nature Titans.
At Very Fast speed, he is devastating. He can fire repeatedly, stacking damage and keeping the enemy team buried under fiends.
Fiends absorb healing, but in Bloody Battle, there is no healing. This makes the fiends (and their debuff) permanent and unremovable for the entire match.
Great in a 3-2 or Mono Red stack to break through Green tanks.
Stacks regular Defense Down (Falcon) with Brimstone's Elemental Defense Down for maximum tile damage.
In Bloody Battle, this trio fills the enemy with fiends that cannot be removed, dealing massive passive damage every turn while reducing their defense.
Ludwig charges them both; Brimstone applies the EDD, and Khufu follows up with a hit that capitalizes on that defense down for a team wipe.
Heroes who specifically destroy fiends or replace them with minions neutralize his main utility instantly.
Can heal enough to remove the fiends quickly.
There is a limit of 3 fiends per hero. If you run too many summoners, you might overwrite Brimstone's specialized EDD fiend with a generic damage fiend.

If you pulled Brimstone early, congratulations! He is a carry hero. Focus on leveling him to max. He will be your main damage dealer for map stages and your ace for Titans. Don't worry too much about his "Core" mechanic; just treat it as bonus damage.

Brimstone is a specialist tool now. You likely have faster heroes for War, but keep him rostered for Mythic Titans and Rush Wars. Is he worth a second Limit Break (Alpha Aether)? Only if you are chasing top 100 Titan scores or lack a modern Red heavy hitter. If you have the latest Goblins or Astral Elves, they might take priority, but Brimstone's EDD keeps him unique.
The community reaction to Brimstone has been a rollercoaster.
Players were shocked by his power. One user noted,
"This guy is insane. If he goes off, you're getting hit for a minimum of 1050... this is stupid strong."
Early on, many called for nerfs, finding his combination of damage and fiends oppressive.
"This hero is way too strong. Either get rid of the core damage or the fiend."
As time passed, players realized his true calling.
"The one good thing about brimstone is that once his EDD is applied, it sticks onto the titan and never wears off. It is imo quite essential for 15

Brimstone is a hero who has aged like fine wine—or perhaps fine magma. While he may not be the terrifying defensive wall he was in 2023, his offensive utility remains top-tier. The ability to apply an uncleansable Elemental Defense Down via a fiend is a mechanic that is hard to replace. He might not be your first choice for a Raid Defense anymore, but on a Titan team or in a Rush war, he is still absolutely lethal.
A Titan essential and a Rush War beast. Keep him hot.
